rationale: The cited paper’s topic (consumers following expert rankings and reacting to invalid information) is plausibly relevant to the citing paper’s core constructs of ranking influence on student choice and strategic information transmission. However, the provided match evidence is only a single DOI reference entry (no in-text citation context or surrounding text), so there is no direct evidence of how substantively it is used. Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop but also not strong enough to clearly keep.
